title: أزولاي يترأس إفطار سلام بالبيضاء

body: المستشار‭ ‬الملكي‭ ‬قال‭ ‬إن‭ ‬المغرب‭ ‬يرسخ‭ ‬واقعا‭ ‬مثيرا‭ ‬للإعجاب قال‭ ‬أندري‭ ‬أزولاي،‭ ‬مستشار‭ ‬الملك‭ ‬محمد‭ ‬السادس،‭ ‬إن‭ “‬المغرب‭ ‬يرسخ‭ ‬جذوره‭ ‬بوضوح‭ ‬وعزيمة‭ ‬في‭ ‬توطيد‭ ‬وتوسيع‭ ‬واقع‭ ‬اجتماعي‭ ‬وثقافي‭ ‬مثير‭ ‬للإعجاب،‭ ‬في‭ ‬مجتمع‭ ‬دولي‭ ‬يبحث‭ ‬أكثر‭ ‬من‭ ‬أي‭ ‬وقت‭ ‬مضى‭ ‬عن‭ ‬نقاط‭ ‬مرجعية،‭ ‬حيث‭ ‬نتطور‭ ‬معا،‭ ‬مع‭ ‬بعضنا‭ أكمل القراءة » المقالة أزولاي يترأس إفطار سلام بالبيضاء fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: مورو يبحث عن مرشح في طنجة

body: لم‭ ‬يحسم‭ ‬التجمع‭ ‬الوطني‭ ‬للأحرار‭ ‬بطنجة‭ ‬الذي‭ ‬يقوده‭ ‬عمر‭ ‬مورو،‭ ‬في‭ ‬الاسم‭ ‬الذي‭ ‬سيقود‭ “‬الحمامة‭” ‬في‭ ‬دائرة‭ ‬طنجة‭ ‬أصيلا،‭ ‬إذ‭ ‬تروج‭ ‬عدة‭ ‬أسماء،‭ ‬ضمنها‭ ‬البرلماني‭ ‬الحالي،‭ ‬ولكن‭ ‬لا‭ ‬شيء‭ ‬حسم،‭ ‬بسبب‭ ‬التدافع‭ ‬و‭”‬شد‭ ‬ليا‭ ‬نقطع‭ ‬ليك‭” ‬داخل‭ ‬فضاء‭ ‬الحزب‭ ‬على‭ ‬مستوى‭ ‬مدينة‭ ‬البوغاز‭.‬ وفي‭ ‬خضم‭ ‬عدم‭ ‬الحسم‭ أكمل القراءة » المقالة مورو يبحث عن مرشح في طنجة fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: 'Truly extraordinary': Mega-laser shooting at us from halfway across the universe is the brightest 'cosmic beacon' we've ever seen
summary: Astronomers have discovered the brightest and most distant "megamaser" to date. The cosmic energy beam is shooting toward Earth from 8 billion light-years away and was spotted thanks to a weird space-time trick first predicted by Einstein.

title: NASA fixes Artemis II rocket for April launch to take astronauts around moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II is on track to shoot for the moon in April after engineers fixed the helium issue that grounded the mission's rocket last month.

title: Birds are declining faster and faster in 3 US hotspots, new study finds
summary: Researchers have revealed that North American birds are declining at an accelerating rate in three regional hotspots associated with intense agriculture.

title: When was the last time Antarctica was ice-free?
summary: Antarctica is covered by a miles-thick ice sheet, but was that always the case? And when was the coldest continent ice-free?

title: Hawke Endurance ED 8x25 binocular review
summary: Lightweight, portable and sharp, the Hawke Endurance ED 8x25 proves compact binoculars can deliver big views without weighing you down.

title: Prehistoric water-dwelling weirdo with sideways teeth and a twisted jaw was already a 'living fossil' 275 million years ago
summary: Scientists have described Tanyka amnicola, a newly identified species of prehistoric creature that lived 275 million years ago and had a bizarre twisted jaw with sideways-facing teeth.

title: Under pressure: the reality of Mexico’s research system
summary:
Nature, Published online: 04 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00442-x

Researcher Oscar Xavier Guerrero Gutiérrez speaks out about the unstable conditions that Mexican scientists face — and what can be done to help.
body: Nature, Published online: 04 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00442-x Researcher Oscar Xavier Guerrero Gutiérrez speaks out about the unstable conditions that Mexican scientists face — and what can be done to help.

title: Mysterious 'little red dots' discovered by James Webb telescope may be the first stars in the universe on the verge of collapse
summary: A new study suggests that "little red dots" spied by the James Webb Space Telescope could be the universe's short-lived first generation of gigantic stars, challenging an existing theory.

title: Stone Age woman was buried like a man, revealing flexible gender roles 7,000 years ago in Hungary
summary: A study of 125 skeletons from two Neolithic cemeteries in Hungary has revealed that men and women had clear gender roles — but sometimes those roles were fluid.

title: 'Collective hum' of black holes could mend our broken understanding of the universe, physicists say
summary: Ripples in the fabric of space-time called gravitational waves may be the key to solving the Hubble tension — one of the biggest nagging problems in physics.

title: 'Blood moon' total lunar eclipse dazzles millions around the world (photos)
summary: Here are the first images of the March 3 'blood moon' total lunar eclipse visible over North America, Australia, and eastern Asia.

title: Every ant is a queen in this parasitic species — and they reproduce by cloning themselves and hijacking other ant colonies
summary: A rare Japanese ant is the only species known to lack female workers and males; all of its young develop into parasitic queens that try to take over other colonies.

title: 3 rivers merge into striking half-and-half waterway in Guyana — Earth from space
summary: A 2023 satellite photo highlights the point where a trio of rivers converges in Guyana. One of the waterways has been significantly altered by mining waste, creating a striking color contrast.

title: Climate research is global — risks and responsibilities should also be distributed
summary:
Nature, Published online: 03 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00680-z

Climate research is global — risks and responsibilities should also be distributed
body: Nature, Published online: 03 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00680-z Climate research is global — risks and responsibilities should also be distributed

title: Ancient Greek mystery cult priestesses may have chemically tweaked fungus to induce psychedelic hallucinations
summary: Ancient followers of the Eleusinian Mysteries may have used a highly toxic fungus to create psychedelic hallucinations during their rituals, a new chemical analysis suggests.

title: March could be the best month for the northern lights for nearly a decade —  if the sun stays active
summary: March 2026 could be the best month for the northern lights until the mid-2030s, as celestial mechanics and solar activity combine for potentially potent results.

title: NASA telescope spots first alien 'astrosphere' around a sun-like star: Space photo of the week
summary: The first bubble of hot gas seen around another star has been spotted around the "Moth," just 117 light-years away.

title: Science history: Stephen Hawking writes a tiny paper — and turns our understanding of black holes inside out — March 1, 1974
summary: In 1974, physicist Stephen Hawking described the potential for tiny, primordial black holes that existed at the dawn of time to explode — and reshaped what we knew about these cosmic behemoths.

title: Giant string of organic molecules on Mars may be one of the best signs of life yet
summary: A new NASA analysis concludes that it is "reasonable to hypothesize" that living things could have formed the odd organic molecules discovered on Mars.

title: 'We're starting to find a lot more weirdness': These strange animals can control their body heat
summary: Some creatures can dramatically alter their internal temperature — a strategy called heterothermy — and outlast storms, floods and predators.

title: Paleolithic humans invented an 'early predecessor to writing' at least 40,000 years ago, carved signs suggest
summary: A statistical analysis of a series of signs carved into artifacts from around 40,000 years ago suggests humans developed proto-writing in the Stone Age.

title: 16 Black-Owned Food Brands That Deserve Space In Your Kitchen
summary: Don't just save your support for February.

title: The sun just experienced its first 'spotless days' in 4 years — but we're not in the clear yet
summary: Earlier this week, the number of visible sunspots on our home star fell to zero for the first time in 1,335 days. This normally indicates a period of greatly reduced solar activity, but it's still too soon to relax, experts say.

title: NASA announces sweeping overhaul of Artemis return to moon, targeting two 2028 landings and a 2027 in-orbit docking flight
summary: A major shakeup to NASA's Artemis program will step rocket launches up to an annual basis, and discard a Boeing-designed upper stage.

title: Just in time for the total lunar eclipse, this beginner-friendly telescope is now $100 off at Amazon
summary: The Celestron StarSense Explorer DX 5-inch Schmidt-Cass is now down to $479 at Amazon, making it easy to enjoy the blood moon eclipse on March 3.

title: Science history: Carbon-14 is discovered, opening a window into past civilizations — Feb. 27, 1940
summary: Martin Kamen and Samuel Ruben's discovery of the radioactive isotope carbon-14 in 1940 helped usher in a new era of dating artifacts from past civilizations.

title: 10 ملايين دولار.. مكافأة أميركية لاعتقال شقيقين يقودان عصابة
summary: قالت وزارة الخارجية الأميركية يوم الخميس إنها ستدفع ما يصل إلى 10 ملايين دولار مقابل معلومات تؤدي إلى اعتقال أو إدانة شقيقين تم تحديدهما كزعيمين لعصابة سينالوا المكسيكية في ولاية باها كاليفورنيا، التي تضم مدينة تيخوانا.

summary:
Nature, Published online: 27 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00538-4

Nicknamed the father of environmental justice, Robert Bullard argues that researchers in the field have more reason than ever to back up their work with …

title: Pokémon turns 30 — how the fictional pocket monsters shaped science
summary:
Nature, Published online: 27 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00441-y

The Japanese media sensation has inspired generations of researchers in fields as diverse as evolution, biodiversity and research integrity.
body: Nature, Published online: 27 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00441-y The Japanese media sensation has inspired generations of researchers in fields as diverse as evolution, biodiversity and research integrity.

title: 'Revolutionary': Vera C. Rubin Observatory found 800,000 objects of interest in a single night
summary: The Vera C. Rubin Observatory sent scientists nearly 1 million astronomy alerts in one night, showing off changes in the sky. Eventually, the telescope is expected to reach 7 million alerts per night.

title: Fresh look at Apollo moon rocks solves decades-old mystery about the moon's magnetic field
summary: The lunar rocks collected by Apollo astronauts suggested the moon had a strong magnetic field. A new analysis shows the opposite.

title: Babies weren't supposed to be mourned in the Roman Empire. These rare liquid-gypsum burials prove otherwise.
summary: Despite historical records saying otherwise, Roman babies were mourned at death, research into unique plaster burials from York reveals.

title: Kazakhstan plants tens of thousands of trees in giant effort to reintroduce tigers
summary: Kazakhstan planted 37,000 seedlings and cuttings in South Balkhash last year to prepare for the return of its tigers, which disappeared more than 70 years ago.

title: We now know why shoes squeak, and it involves miniature lightning bolts
summary: Harvard engineers think they've found the reason basketball shoes squeak, and it's due to pockets of friction between the rubber and the court.

title: تأثير إبستين يصل إلى "مؤتمر الديناصورات".. ماذا حدث؟
summary: قرر مؤتمر بريطاني متخصص في بحث تاريخ الديناصورات، منع حضور بعض علماء الحفريات المذكورين في ملفات جيفري إبستين، رجل الأعمال الأميركي المدان بانتهاكات جنسية.

title: في فيلم أميركي ضخم.. مشهد عن الأهرامات يثير غضب المصريين
summary: أثار مقطع من واحد من أشهر الأفلام الأميركية هذا العام، استياء عدد كبير من المصريين، بسبب معلومة تتعلق ببناء الأهرامات.
